slides.lead: Demo (sample) presentation of the template. All text is placeholder (dummy) and exists only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. The event name, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom go here. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared shipping goal.
slides.notes: Team slide. Instead of photos it uses initials in a colored circle, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions in between.
slides.notes: Agenda as three big tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Player retention, build health and our biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Big heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Telemetry, reviews and playtests capture more than any team can read. Value appears only when we connect it.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: big heading, bold illustration, short caption. Great for a key point or a chapter.

items: day-30 retention after the onboarding rework
saved weekly on manual build packaging
fewer crash reports after the stability patch
bullets: Measure impact, not activity: the effect on players is what counts.
Start where you already have data and where the pain is loudest.
A small build with a clear metric beats a big plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a caption, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The loop does not wait for churn, it predicts it.
bullets: Session length, drop-off points and spend signals.
What preceded past churn and quiet quitting.
Before a cohort silently slips away.
The next event into the window that lands best.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself up.

slides.lead: Same scope, two approaches and two burnout curves.
cards: Ship by sprinting until the team breaks. Costly and unpredictable.
Cut features to hit the date. Better, but often cuts the wrong ones.
Ship small slices on a steady rhythm. Lowest long-term cost.
Critical milestones on cadence, the rest flexible.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a modern studio.
cards: Automated builds for every platform, close to commit.
Real-time view of player behavior and anomalies.
Flags unusual stability dips before reviews do.
Turns the changelog into player-ready notes.
Keeps the match fair and your data under control.
A team players trust and actually talk to.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for a tools or capability overview.

items: Growth slows, so it pays to polish the core loop.
A creator picks it up and the player base spikes fast.
Seasonal content keeps the world alive year after year.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: The studio that wins is not the one with the most ideas, but the one that turns a build into a decision the fastest.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under deadline.
bullets: Every feature carries the name of a person who answers for it.
You know in advance how you will tell it worked.
On core loops, real playtests beat opinions.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A build without a metric and an owner ends up shelved.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one big sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.h1: Patch notes from data in 30 seconds.
slides.lead: We switch into the tool and generate today's build summary live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
